rationale: The citation is used in the methods section to justify how media-message variables are constructed, not just as generic background. The authors explicitly follow Chevalier and Mayzlin (2006) in operationalizing message volume (count of messages) and valence (positive minus negative messages), and they also invoke it to support excluding message length once volume/content are controlled. This indicates a concrete methodological transfer into the current study's media-coverage context.
relation_description: The citing paper applies measurement ideas from Chevalier and Mayzlin (2006) to its own media-content data, using analogous volume and valence constructions and related modeling choices. The cited paper thus serves as a methodological template for message coding decisions in a different empirical setting.
